suggestions.title: 118 @keyframes definitions across CSS files — animation budget is out of control
suggestions.description: Office.css has 90 @keyframes and App.css has 28 — totaling 118 animation definitions. Many are duplicates or near-duplicates (dust-drift defined twice, ghost-float used in multiple places). The browser has to parse and keep all of these in memory. Consolidate duplicates and remove unused keyframes. This directly impacts paint performance on low-end devices.

suggestions.title: Office.css is 2071 lines — needs splitting or CSS modules
suggestions.description: A single 2071-line CSS file is unmaintainable. Combined with App.css at 1082 lines, that's 3153 lines of global CSS with potential selector collisions. Consider CSS modules, or at minimum split Office.css into desk.css, character.css, animations.css, etc.

suggestions.title: Coffee corner vibe-text selectors rely on data-vibe attribute on ancestor — verify propagation
suggestions.description: Lines 1367-1371 of Office.css use [data-vibe='crushing'] .coffee-corner__label selectors. If the data-vibe attribute isn't set on a parent element that contains the coffee corner, these styles silently fail. Verified this was flagged in QA #17 and may still be broken depending on DOM structure. Need to verify the data-vibe attribute is on the office-floor wrapper.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard memo defeated by activityEntries array prop
suggestions.description: WorkerCard (App.jsx:226) uses React.memo but receives activityEntries as a filtered array. Every render creates a new array reference, defeating memoization. Use useMemo to stabilize the filtered array or pass a stable selector.

suggestions.title: Characters have tabIndex=0 but no keyboard interaction handler
suggestions.description: Office.jsx:508 sets tabIndex={0} on characters, making them focusable, but keyboard events (Enter/Space) should trigger the same action as click. Verify onKeyDown handler exists for the character div.

suggestions.title: stableActivityLog useMemo is a no-op
suggestions.description: App.jsx:746 — `useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog])` does literally nothing. useMemo with the same reference as dependency returns the same reference. This is dead code that suggests a misunderstanding of memoization.

suggestions.title: Reduced-motion selectors are thorough but some target removed animations
suggestions.description: There are 16 prefers-reduced-motion blocks across both CSS files. Some target animations that may have been removed or renamed in recent refactors (e.g., Office.css:1231 nullifies dust-drift and ghost-float in a reduced-motion block but the animations are defined elsewhere and used). Audit each block against actual animation usage.

suggestions.title: No loading skeleton or placeholder when workers.json fetch is in-flight
suggestions.description: When the page first loads or when polling fails, there's no visual indication. A skeleton loader for the office view would prevent the flash of empty content.

suggestions.title: DESK_SCREEN_SVG object in Office.jsx is 60+ lines of inline SVG
suggestions.description: Lines 8-63 of Office.jsx define static SVG screens as JSX. These could be extracted to separate SVG files or a dedicated component file to reduce Office.jsx complexity.

suggestions.title: Activity log has no local persistence — refreshing loses context
suggestions.description: Activity entries are fetched from GitHub raw but not cached in localStorage. A page refresh during a deploy means the user loses the activity timeline until the next fetch succeeds.

suggestions.title: Skip-link exists but verify it targets the right anchor
suggestions.description: App.css:17 styles a skip-link. Verify the href points to a valid #main-content id that exists in the DOM.
